summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates installer.env contents and host metadata to ngrok tunnel
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall.js unconditionally reads the installer's `.env` file from INIT_CWD, extracts entries whose keys start with `DEMO_`, and POSTs them together with `os.hostname()` and `os.platform()` to a hardcoded ngrok-free.dev URL (`https://scary-blooper-brewery.ngrok-free.dev/collect`). ngrok-free.dev hostnames are ephemeral, operator-controlled tunnels — a classic shape for ad-hoc collection infrastructure, not legitimate vendor telemetry. The package additionally ships an empty `scripts/mine_cyrpto.js` referenced by the `build` script, a suggestively-named placeholder wired into the package without any current content.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
